name: Mcilwain School Bus Lines Inc
districts_or_customers: operations in Windber, Johnstown, Berlin, Somerset, and Cresson PA
recent_news: Third-generation family business; Douglas McIlwain has owned since 1991 with three sons (Patrick, Timothy, Toby) in leadership roles — succession path appears established but family-owned, no public M&A activity noted as of 2026.
fit_notes: Highly attractive Summit tuck-in: scaled 300-bus western PA platform serving 10,000+ students daily across 90+ schools, with five depot locations. Third-gen family business with multiple sons in management means succession could go either way, but size, contract density, and geographic concentration in PA (a Summit state) make this a premium target if family becomes open to sale.

name: Franmar School Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: private schools in Miami-Dade, Broward, and Monroe counties
recent_news: Acquired by Beacon Mobility (Audax-backed) on March 1, 2023; Cassel Salpeter served as financial advisor on sale. Franmar continues operating under its brand as part of Beacon's Florida platform.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in candidate — already owned by competing PE-backed platform (Beacon Mobility / Audax). Could be relevant for competitive intel on FL market positioning; would only become available via Beacon-level divestiture, not direct outreach.

name: Eshelman Transportation Inc
recent_news: 3rd/4th generation family business; over 70 years of pupil transportation; operates from four Berks County locations. No public M&A activity. Gregg Eshelman also serves on Berks County Agricultural Land Preservation Board.
fit_notes: Strong Summit tuck-in candidate: 100-bus Berks County PA operator with 5 named district contracts and 4 depot locations. Multi-generational family ownership with Preston Eshelman as next-gen Sr Manager suggests succession may already be progressing internally, but 90+ year history and Gregg's age/civic activities (next-gen visible) could surface a sale window. PA is a Summit core state.

name: Academy Business Co Inc
districts_or_customers: schools in Yonkers/Westchester County area (specific contracts not publicly disclosed)
recent_news: Long-tenured Yonkers operator (~65 years in business as of 2026) under single owner Robert Sturtevant; not affiliated with the larger NJ-based Academy Bus Lines. No public M&A activity. A+ BBB rating, not accredited.
fit_notes: Plausible Summit tuck-in: 65-year-old single-owner Yonkers/Westchester operator under Bob Sturtevant — long-tenured solo principal flags strong succession potential. Westchester County is a dense, contract-rich market adjacent to Summit's NY/Tri-State footprint. Size is modest but proximity and clear owner-driven succession dynamic make it worth a direct outreach.

name: Tri Star Transport
service_types: freight/property hauling (building materials, machinery) — NOT confirmed as school bus operator
recent_news: FMCSA SAFER (USDOT #2003733) lists Tri Star Transport Corp at 335 Bloomingburg Rd, Middletown NY as a Private(Property) intrastate freight carrier with only 1 power unit and 2 drivers — not authorized for passenger/school bus operations. Source list label of 'school=1' and 60 employees appears to be a data-quality error. Middletown NY school transportation is contracted to Mid-City Transit, not Tri Star.
fit_notes: NOT a fit — appears to be a small freight/property hauler (1 truck, 2 drivers), not a school bus operator. Website tristartransport.com belongs to an unrelated TN-based hazmat carrier. Recommend deleting from Summit prospect list as miscategorized.
